content.finding: Single-K diagnostics (K=6 only) have CV≈14.8%. Multi-K averaging across K=[4,6,8,10] achieves CV≈5.1%, a 3x variance reduction.
content.impact: This methodological upgrade enables detection of binding effects that are invisible to single-K diagnostics.
content.example: Clip bounds appeared non-binding at K=6 (NARROW_TIGHT d=0.610, p=0.096, CV=14.8%) but are clearly binding at multi-K (VERY_NARROW d=7.583, p=0.000, CV=5.1%).
content.implication: All Phase 1 diagnostics must use multi-K protocol going forward to avoid false negatives.
content.protocol: For each condition: evaluate at K in [4,6,8,10], compute MI gap per K, average across K values to reduce noise.
